title: mammillaria geminata subsp. beiselii
intro: Mammillaria geminata subsp. beiselii, a rare and lesser-known cactus species, is a subunit of the Mammillaria geminata plant, characterized by its small, globular shape and vibrant green color. Traditionally, the plant has been used in folk medicine, particularly in Mexico, where it is valued for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic qualities. The plant is believed to offer various health benefits, including wound healing, skin protection, and anti-arthritic properties, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy. In herbal preparations, Mammillaria geminata subsp. beiselii is typically harvested, dried, and processed into infusions, decoctions, or topical creams to harness its medicinal properties.
